## [00:00 - 03:00]

This is something most people don't think about. Learning how to handle high-stakes situations is a critical skill. In this video, I'm gonna teach you two ways to de-escalate volatile situations. One of the most important rules and one of the most powerful rules in communication is this idea of matching and mirroring. If someone comes up to you and they've got very big body language and you play with very small body language, well, then they're not gonna feel as connected to you. And the idea behind matching and mirroring is because if we look like someone and we act like someone, well, then we have a lot in common already and therefore there's gonna be more rapport. Now, matching and mirroring, you can do this with your body language. You can match and mirror someone. And you can also match and mirror someone with your vocal foundations. And I'm not saying you being fake and then all of a sudden you're putting on a weird accent because the other person has one as well. No, no, no, I'm not talking about that. So if someone comes up to you, they use big body language, you now start to use big body language too. They're gonna feel in more rapport with you. And then if they're using a strong and loud voice and then you also appear with a strong and loud voice and use big body language, then that person's gonna think, well, this person seems a lot like me.
